§ Learning

What I’m chasing.

A standing log of what I am trying to get better at, why it feels worth the time, and the resources I keep returning to. Updated quarterly.

Screen-reader voice design

· Active

I want our announcements to read like a tactful colleague, not a server log. That means understanding the grammar screen readers impose, and where we can gently disobey it.

  • BookAccessibility for Everyone— Laura Kalbag
  • CourseDeque University — Screen Readers— Deque
  • TalkWhat the heck is ARIA?— Eric Bailey
  • PaperAnnouncing Intent in Dynamic UIs— Internal WIP

Perceptual contrast (beyond WCAG)

· Active

WCAG 2.x contrast ratios predate most of the design we now ship. APCA and the work around it is finally giving us a better measuring stick. I want to know where it still falls short.

  • ReadingAPCA working draft— Andrew Somers
  • ReadingReadability Algorithms Compared— Stripe Engineering
  • ToolSim Daltonism— Michel Fortin

Management as craft

· Ongoing

The longer I manage, the more I think it is a distinct craft rather than a promotion from design. Trying to read and practice accordingly.

  • BookResilient Management— Lara Hogan
  • BookAn Elegant Puzzle— Will Larson
  • LetterLeadDev— Various

Static type systems for UI

· Dabbling

I have been writing more TypeScript again this year. I want to stop defaulting to "any" when I reach the edges of my understanding.

  • BookEffective TypeScript— Dan Vanderkam
  • CourseTotal TypeScript — Beginner— Matt Pocock

Low-vision empathy (not simulation)

· Backburner

Simulators lie. I want to spend more time with low-vision users directly, and less time squinting through a filter and pretending I understand.

  • CommunityFable Tech Labs— Fable
  • EventCSUN assistive tech conference— CSUN
Design with Thought

A writing-and-projects site by Brian Hinton. Essays arrive slowly. Notes, more often.
